mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered months despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that lifts equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization that generates short-term earnings surprises and positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index breaching 235.0 triggers inflation risk premium repricing that compresses equity multiples and produces negative monthly forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 235.0 coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth directly increases aggregate wage income and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns with t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth outpacing quarterly GDP growth signals unsustainable consumption leverage that triggers short-term mean reversion and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS to GDPC1 divergence predicts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stagnant unemployment during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints that suppress profit margins and drive negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO and UNRATE divergence yields positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered quarters.
